threats.name: Phishing Attack
threats.description: Attackers send fake emails pretending to be trusted organisations to steal login credentials or personal information.
threats.affected: Email users, banking customers, corporate employees
threats.prevention: Never click suspicious links. Always verify the sender's email address.

threats.name: Ransomware
threats.description: Malicious software that encrypts your files and demands payment to restore access.
threats.prevention: Keep regular backups. Never open attachments from unknown senders.

threats.name: SQL Injection
threats.description: Attackers insert malicious SQL code into web forms to gain unauthorised access to databases.
threats.affected: Websites with login forms, e-commerce platforms
threats.prevention: Use parameterised queries. Keep software updated.

threats.name: Man-in-the-Middle Attack
threats.description: An attacker secretly intercepts and possibly alters communications between two parties.
threats.affected: Public Wi-Fi users, online banking customers
threats.prevention: Avoid public Wi-Fi. Always use HTTPS websites. Use a VPN.

threats.name: Brute Force Attack
threats.description: Attackers try every possible password combination until they find the correct one.
threats.affected: Any account protected by a weak password
threats.prevention: Use long, complex passwords. Enable two-factor authentication.

threats.name: Spyware
threats.description: Software that secretly monitors your activity, collecting passwords, browsing history and personal data.
threats.prevention: Install reputable antivirus software. Avoid downloading from untrusted sources.

threats.name: DDoS Attack
threats.description: Attackers flood a server with traffic to make a website or service unavailable to real users.
threats.prevention: Use DDoS protection services. Monitor traffic for unusual spikes.

threats.name: Keylogger
threats.description: Software that secretly records every keystroke you make, capturing passwords and sensitive information.
threats.prevention: Use antivirus software. Avoid using unknown computers for sensitive tasks.

threats.name: Zero-Day Exploit
threats.description: An attack that targets a previously unknown software vulnerability before developers have had a chance to fix it.
threats.affected: Any software users, especially those who delay updates
threats.prevention: Always install software updates immediately. Use vulnerability scanning tools.

threats.name: Identity Theft
threats.description: Criminals steal personal information such as name, address and financial details to commit fraud.
threats.affected: General public, online shoppers, social media users
threats.prevention: Monitor your accounts regularly. Use strong unique passwords for every account.

threats.name: Adware
threats.description: Unwanted software that displays excessive advertisements and can slow down your device.
threats.prevention: Download software only from official sources. Use an ad blocker.

threats.name: Credential Stuffing
threats.description: Attackers use lists of stolen usernames and passwords from one breach to try to access other accounts.
threats.affected: People who reuse passwords across multiple sites
threats.prevention: Use a unique password for every account. Use a password manager.
